methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $3.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Astranis, a Series D space and aerospace company founded by John Gedmark and Ryan McLinko, has raised $350M+ and carries a post-money valuation of $3.5B. The valuation analysis draws from two applicable methods anchored to a comp universe of 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (including Apex Space, Varda Space Industries, and Sierra Space) and 5 public space & aerospace benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $3.5B last post-money valuation, yielding $5.2B. Peer-median triangulation across the 8-company private cohort produces $1.1B, though this peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, ranging from $1.0B (Apex Space, Series D) to $8.0B (Sierra Space, Series C). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median: 36.9x across 6 tickers) is inapplicable due to absence of disclosed revenue figures. A three-point triangulation—incorporating the $914M floor (discounted peer median), $3.2B median, and $6.0B ceiling (stage-progression upside)—reflects material divergence between methodologies. The current $3.5B valuation sits near the midpoint but substantially above peer-median estimates, suggesting either sector-relative execution premium or limited comparability within the private comp set. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory disclosure and the relevance of high-valuation peers (Sierra Space at $8.0B) operating at materially different stages or business maturity.
